title: How fabio handles API throttling
summary: fabio transparently handles 429 (Too Many Requests) and gateway errors. Agents do NOT need to implement retry logic.

fabio_behavior.rate_limit_429: fabio retries automatically using the Retry-After header value (capped at 60s). Parallel operations (upload, sync, copy) use bounded concurrency with rate-limit retry.
fabio_behavior.gateway_errors_502_503_504: Automatic retry with linear backoff (1s, 2s, 3s) for up to 3 attempts.
fabio_behavior.capacity_throttle_430: Spark operations on small capacity may return 430 (TooManyRequestsForCapacity). fabio reports this as RATE_LIMITED error.

what_agents_should_do: Nothing — fabio handles all retries transparently
If you get RATE_LIMITED error, the operation already exhausted retries. Wait and try again later.
Use --verbose to see HTTP response codes and retry behavior on stderr
For bulk operations, fabio uses bounded parallelism (default 8 concurrent) with automatic backoff

relevant_flags.--verbose: Shows HTTP status codes and retry attempts on stderr
relevant_flags.--concurrency N: Control parallelism for bulk/parallel operations (lower = less throttling)

error_codes.RATE_LIMITED: API returned 429 after all retries exhausted. Wait before retrying.
error_codes.TIMEOUT: LRO polling exceeded --lro-timeout (default 120s). Operation may still be running.
